title: C Type Conversion
content:
### Type Conversion in C

Type conversion is converting one data type to another. C supports both implicit and explicit conversion.

### Implicit Conversion (Automatic)

C automatically converts smaller types to larger types when needed (type promotion).

```
Conversion Hierarchy: char -> short -> int -> unsigned int -> long -> float -> double
```

### Explicit Conversion (Casting)

You can force a conversion using the cast operator:

```c
(type_name) expression
```

### Common Conversions

| Conversion | Example | Result |
|------------|---------|--------|
| int to float | `(float) 5` | `5.0` |
| float to int | `(int) 3.9` | `3` (truncates) |
| double to float | `(float) 3.14159` | loses precision |
| char to int | `(int) 'A'` | `65` (ASCII) |

---

> **Key Point:** Implicit conversion follows the hierarchy. Explicit casting is needed when converting from larger to smaller types or when you need specific truncation behavior.
